mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 jobs signals robust labor market expansion, which increases consumer purchasing power and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 159540 thousand as of April 2014 indicates sustained employment growth
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with stable labor market expansion
failure_condition: Rank IC of PAYEMS momentum signal falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll months fail to produce positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.50% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows no acceleration above 2.5% year-over-year, the real policy rate is deeply negative, signaling monetary accommodation that compresses equity risk premiums and produces positive forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.22% and CPIAUCSL at 323.364 as of May 2014
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with contained inflation expectations
failure_condition: The interaction signal fails to produce positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term is not significantly higher than either component alone (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,200 thousand units signals weakening residential demand and forward-looking economic weakness, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1307.0 thousand as of April 2014
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with housing market normalization
failure_condition: Housing declines fail to produce negative 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerating above 0.5% month-over-month signals strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side expansion, which supports corporate profit margins and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.9203 as of April 2014 reflects post-crisis manufacturing recovery
regime_hint: post-crisis supply-side recovery with improving capacity utilization
failure_condition: INDPRO momentum fails to predict positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) both declining simultaneously signals a synchronized demand shock across consumer and residential sectors, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as earnings expectations compress across multiple end-markets.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST declines > 3% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 732010.0 million and HOUST at 1307.0 thousand as of April 2014
regime_hint: demand-constrained environment with synchronized weakness across consumer and housing
failure_condition: The joint demand shock signal fails to produce negative 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) when tested against single-series alternatives.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and forward-looking recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors reprice terminal growth rates and equity risk premiums widen.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points in a 3-month window
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.3% as of April 2014 indicates tight labor market
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market tightening reversing
failure_condition: Unemployment rate increases fail to produce negative 60-day returns in > 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the unemployment signal is below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) rising above 4.0% while real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains below 2.5% annualized signals monetary policy tightening into economic weakness, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as the market reprices stagflation risk.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.22% and GDPC1 at 23770.976 billion as of late 2013/early 2014
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with slowing growth
failure_condition: The policy-growth mismatch signal fails to produce negative 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term does not explain variance beyond FEDFUNDS alone (R² improvement < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) rises simultaneously signals a supply-side shock combined with labor market weakness, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors immediately reprice earnings and growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.3% month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.9203 and UNRATE at 4.3% as of April 2014
regime_hint: supply-side shock environment with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: The combined supply-labor shock signal fails to produce negative 5-day returns in > 62% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect is not statistically distinguishable from the sum of individual effects (p > 0.20).
